metric_data.name: invest_quality [GEval]
metric_data.reason: The Actual Output respects the required format and replaces vague terms with measurable conditions, including a specific acceptance criterion with an action verb and measurable outcome. It also adheres to the INVEST framework and preserves the original actor/role and business intent from the Input. However, it only includes one acceptance criterion and lacks a second, which prevents it from fully meeting the evaluation criteria.
metric_data.verboseLogs:
Criteria:
Evaluate whether ACTUAL_OUTPUT (the improved user story) is genuinely better than INPUT (the original user story) according to the INVEST framework. Award a HIGH score (close to 1.0) when ALL of the following are true:
  (1) Format respected: 'As a [role], I want [feature], so that [benefit]'
  (2) Vague terms (quickly, easily, seamless, better, fast) are replaced with measurable conditions
  (3) At least 2 acceptance criteria with action verbs AND measurable outcomes (e.g. 'within 2s', 'minimum 6 characters', 'at least 3 items')
  (4) INVEST respected: Independent (no dependency on other stories), Negotiable (no technology imposed), Valuable (clear business benefit), Estimable (specific enough), Small (one sprint), Testable
  (5) Original actor/role and business intent preserved — no new features invented
Award a LOW score (close to 0.0) when the output is identical to the input, still vague, or violates the original intent. 
 
Evaluation Steps:
[
    "Compare the format of INPUT and ACTUAL_OUTPUT to ensure ACTUAL_OUTPUT respects the 'As a [role], I want [feature], so that [benefit]' format",
    "Evaluate if ACTUAL_OUTPUT replaces vague terms from INPUT with measurable conditions and includes at least 2 acceptance criteria with action verbs and measurable outcomes",
    "Assess if ACTUAL_OUTPUT adheres to the INVEST framework and preserves the original actor/role and business intent from INPUT",
    "Determine the score based on the extent to which ACTUAL_OUTPUT improves upon INPUT, awarding a high score if all criteria are met and a low score if the output is identical, vague, or violates the original intent"
] 
 
Rubric:
None 
 
Score: 0.8
